tool_args.label: FMTAC has significant risk of material adverse deviation

payload.claim_text: There is a significant risk of material adverse deviation from the carried net reserve amounts for FMTAC due to the long-tailed nature of liability exposures and the potential for large claims under the Excess Loss Program.
payload.subject: First Mutual Transportation Assurance Company

payload.claim_text: Materiality standard of 15% of total capital and surplus, or $27,910,707, selected for determining risk of material adverse deviation.

tool_args.label: LIRR New Hyde Park Derailment Claims

payload.claim_text: On October 8, 2016, work being performed on the LIRR track in the vicinity of New Hyde Park Station resulted in a train derailment. 72 claims total: 57 passenger injuries, 8 employee injuries, remaining property damage. $11 million FMTAC Force Account retention limit exhausted. $7.9 million outstanding reserves. 4 open lawsuits.

On January 4, 2017, LIRR Far Rockaway Line train struck a bumper block at Atlantic Terminal-Brooklyn Station resulting in 173 injury claims. LIRR paid entire $11 million and $19.7 million impacted ELF program. One claim remaining with $200 thousand reserve.

On April 10, 2016, Robert Liciaga rode bicycle through cordoned-off construction site beneath elevated subway line and was struck by rotted cross tie. Kings County Supreme Court jury found MTA NYCT 100% liable. Award reduced from $110 million to $69 million. Settlement reached for $65 million. FMTAC contributed $31 million.
